title: Pavan K Reddy Smashes Century as Blasters of Bharat Hold Nerve in One-Run Thriller
author: Blasters of Bharat Admin Team
description: Pavan K Reddy's breathtaking 104 off 48 balls powered Blasters of Bharat to 221, but they had to defend 17 runs in the final over against Star strikers, clinching a one-run victory under Sachin Mishra's captaincy.

content.value: A Sunday to Remember: Century, Nerves, and a One-Run Victory

Some matches are won by runs. Others are won by character. For Blasters of Bharat, this Sunday was a testament to both. Led by captain Sachin Mishra, the team found themselves in a high-octane contest against Star strikers at the Chase Cricket Academy in Bengaluru, where individual brilliance met collective resilience. A breathtaking century from Pavan K Reddy (PKR) powered them to a mammoth 221, but it was their death-bowling nerves that sealed a dramatic one-run victory after Star strikers needed 17 runs off the final over — a finish that had everyone on the edge of their seats.
First Innings – Pavan K Reddy's Explosive Masterclass

Opting to bat first after winning the toss, Blasters of Bharat lost captain Sachin Mishra early for 8 off 8 balls. RANDEEP R followed soon after for 9. Then came Abhinav Upadhyay at number three, who played a steady hand with 3 off 4 balls before falling to Nawaz M K. But the real carnage was about to begin.

Pavan K Reddy walked in and proceeded to dismantle the Star strikers' bowling attack with ruthless efficiency. His 104 off just 48 balls was a knock for the ages — 15 boundaries and 4 towering sixes, striking at an incredible 216.67. He reached his half-century off just 21 balls and brought up his century off 45 deliveries.

He found able partners in Kapil Sk, who played a brilliant supporting hand with 48 off 30 balls (3 fours, 4 sixes). Together, they added 100 runs for the third wicket in just 45 balls, completely shifting the momentum. R Ajay contributed a quick 17 off 11 balls, and Dhanashekar remained unbeaten on 10 off 5 balls. The middle overs saw fireworks galore, with Blasters of Bharat crossing the 200-run mark with ease. They eventually finished at 221/10 in 19.5 overs, setting Star strikers a daunting target.

For Star strikers, Nawaz M K was the pick of the bowlers, claiming 5 wickets for 41 runs in his 3.5 overs — a brilliant five-wicket haul despite the onslaught. Amit Gupta picked up 2 wickets for 28 runs in his 4 overs, while gangadhar reddy and Praneeth chipped in with one wicket each. However, the damage had already been done by Pavan Reddy's breathtaking century.
The Chase – A Nail-Biting Finish

Chasing 222, Star strikers began their innings with intent. Naveen Tattin smashed 23 off 12 balls, but the bowling attack of Blasters of Bharat kept striking at regular intervals. R Ajay was the standout bowler, claiming 3 wickets for just 23 runs in his 4 overs, including a maiden over. Suman Kumar Singh also impressed with 3 wickets for 33 runs in his 4 overs.

Abhishek S G played a brilliant counter-attacking knock for Star strikers, scoring 54 off 32 balls (6 fours, 3 sixes). Vinod Chimpula added 34 off 35 balls, keeping the chase alive. With the equation coming down to the final over, Star strikers needed 17 runs off the last 6 balls with two wickets in hand.
The Final Over – A Rollercoaster of Emotions

With 17 runs needed off the final over, the tension at the Chase Cricket Academy was unbearable. Vidhya Sagar bowled the final over for Blasters of Bharat, defending a target that seemed just out of reach. What followed was a sequence that no one in the crowd would ever forget.

The over began with two wides, immediately putting pressure on the bowler. A no-ball followed, and then a four — suddenly the equation had come down dramatically. With each ball, the required runs kept shrinking. Rohit and Nawaz M K fought hard, taking singles and twos, keeping the chase alive.

With 1 run needed off the final ball, the crowd held its breath. Vidhya Sagar delivered a perfect delivery, and the batters could only manage a single. Star strikers fell agonizingly short, finishing at 220/9 in their 20 overs. Blasters of Bharat had won by one run — defending 17 runs in the final over in a finish for the ages.
Key Performances That Made the Difference

For Blasters of Bharat, Pavan K Reddy was the undisputed star, his 104 off 48 balls setting the platform for the big total. Kapil Sk's 48 off 30 provided crucial support, while Abhinav Upadhyay chipped in with a steady 3 off 4. R Ajay contributed with both bat (17 off 11) and ball (3 wickets for 23 runs). Suman Kumar Singh's 3 wickets for 33 runs kept the chase in check. Vidhya Sagar held his nerve in the final over, defending 17 runs to seal the victory.

For Star strikers, Abhishek S G's 54 off 32 balls and Vinod Chimpula's 34 off 35 kept them in the hunt. Naveen Tattin's quickfire 23 off 12 gave them a flying start. However, their chase was hampered by 54 extras conceded by Blasters of Bharat, including 35 wides and 10 no-balls — a statistic that nearly cost them the match but ultimately wasn't enough.
Turning Point – The Final Over Heroics

Without a doubt, the turning point of the match was Vidhya Sagar's final over. With 17 runs needed off 6 balls and the opposition smelling victory, he held his nerve to deliver a spell that will be remembered for a long time. Despite wides, no-balls, and boundaries, he ensured that Star strikers fell one run short of the target. It was a testament to mental strength under the highest pressure.
A Victory Under Sachin Mishra's Captaincy

This match also marked a significant moment for captain Sachin Mishra. Leading from the front with the bat and making crucial decisions in the field, he guided his team to a victory that showcased their fighting spirit. Defending a total under pressure is never easy, but Blasters of Bharat showed they have the temperament to handle high-stakes situations. The one-run win is a testament to the team's collective resolve and belief.
What This Victory Means for Blasters of Bharat

This win goes beyond the two points. It's about proving to themselves that they can defend totals under pressure, even when the opposition needs 17 runs off the final over. It's about having a player like Pavan K Reddy who can single-handedly take the game away from the opposition. It's about bowlers like Vidhya Sagar who can hold their nerve in the final over. Most importantly, it's about building a winning culture under captain Sachin Mishra.

For Star strikers, this was a heartbreaking loss — coming so close to pulling off a miraculous chase only to fall short by a single run despite a valiant effort from Abhishek S G and Nawaz M K's five-wicket haul. But such defeats often become the foundation for future success. The experience of a high-pressure chase will be invaluable for their development.
Looking Ahead

With this thrilling one-run victory under their belt, Blasters of Bharat will look to build on this momentum. Pavan K Reddy's century has added a new dimension to their batting, and the team's ability to defend under pressure will give them confidence in future matches. The future looks bright for this determined side as they continue to grow under Sachin Mishra's leadership.
Full Match Scorecard

title: Blasters of Bharat Script History: First Successful Run Chase Seals Thrilling Victory
author: BOB Admin Team
description: In a nerve-wracking finish, Blasters of Bharat chased down 141 runs against Forever 11, marking their first successful run chase in dramatic fashion with a final-over Mankad dismissal sealing the victory.

content.value: A Day to Remember: Blasters of Bharat's First Successful Chase

In the annals of Blasters of Bharat's cricketing journey, some matches are just games. And then there are matches that become folklore. This was one of those mornings. Chasing 141 runs against Forever 11 at the Bangalore Cricket Ground - BCG2, Blasters of Bharat scripted a dramatic victory that will be talked about for years to come. It wasn't just a win — it was their first successful run chase in the team's history, achieved in the most nail-biting fashion imaginable, sealed by a controversial Mankad dismissal in the final over.
First Innings – Forever 11 Post a Competitive Total

Batting first after winning the toss, Forever 11 posted a respectable 140 runs in their 20 overs, losing all 10 wickets. The innings was anchored by a gritty 43 off 32 balls from Chani, who held the middle order together with a well-constructed knock that included 4 boundaries and a six. B.Veera Shivaji contributed a steady 31 off 31 balls at the top, while Sathish Angelov added 19 off 19 balls.

For Blasters of Bharat, Pavan_K_Reddy (PKR) was the pick of the bowlers, claiming 2 wickets for 21 runs in his 4 overs. Suman Kumar Singh also impressed with 2 wickets for 23 runs in his 4 overs, while Kushagra Singh picked up 2 wickets for 20 runs in his 3 overs. Kapil Sk chipped in with 1 wicket for 22 runs. The bowling attack was disciplined, with Pavan_K_Reddy (PKR) being named Player of the Match for his all-round contribution.
The Chase – A Rollercoaster of Emotions

If the first innings was about consolidation, the second innings was about heart-stopping drama. Blasters of Bharat began their chase of 141 with intent. Abhinav Upadhyay looked in sublime touch, smashing 4 boundaries in his 26 off just 20 balls, while Kushagra Singh played the anchor role with 12 off 21 balls. Pavan_K_Reddy (PKR) added a quick 26 off 20 balls, keeping the chase on track. Kapil Sk provided the late fireworks with a blistering 40 off 26 balls, including 5 boundaries and a six.

But Forever 11's bowlers refused to give up. B.Veera Shivaji was the pick of their bowlers, claiming 2 wickets for just 18 runs in his 4 overs, including the crucial wicket of Kapil Sk at a critical juncture. Chani opened the bowling with a wicket-maiden and finished with economical figures of 1 for 19 in his 4 overs. B.Sasidhar also made his mark with 2 wickets for 35 runs in 3.4 overs, including the game-changing moment.
The Final Over – A Finish for the Ages

With 6 runs needed off the final over, the tension at the ground was palpable. Sagar Sharma and Vidhya Sagar were at the crease, with B.Sasidhar handed the ball for the decisive over. What followed was a sequence that no one in the crowd would ever forget.

The first ball was a wide, giving the Blasters a lifeline. Sagar Sharma then smashed the next ball for three runs, bringing the equation down to just 2 runs off 5 balls. The pressure was mounting. Then came the moment that would define the match.
The Mankad That Sealed History

On the third ball of the over, with the batters looking to steal runs and keep the scoreboard ticking, B.Sasidhar ran out Sagar Sharma at the non-striker's end in a Mankad dismissal. The ground erupted in mixed reactions — celebration from Blasters of Bharat, shock and debate from the Forever 11 camp. Sagar Sharma was dismissed for a well-made 16 off just 11 balls, leaving the equation at 2 runs needed off 3 balls.

With the wicket falling, the new batter Sai Murali walked in with 2 runs needed off 3 balls. The very next ball, Sai Murali smashed a boundary off the first delivery he faced, taking Blasters of Bharat to 144 runs and sealing the victory. The final scorecard read 144/7 in 19.4 overs — Blasters of Bharat had chased down their first target in team history.
Key Performances That Made the Difference

For Blasters of Bharat, Kapil Sk was the star with the bat, smashing 40 off 26 balls (5 fours, 1 six) in a crucial cameo. Abhinav Upadhyay's 26 off 20 gave them a flying start, while Pavan_K_Reddy (PKR) contributed with both bat (26 off 20) and ball (2 for 21), earning him the Player of the Match award. Kushagra Singh anchored the chase with 12 off 21 balls. Sagar Sharma played a vital hand with 16 off 11 balls, keeping the chase on track before the dramatic dismissal. Vidhya Sagar remained unbeaten on 1 off 7 balls, and Sai Murali finished the game in style with a boundary off the very first ball he faced.

For Forever 11, B.Veera Shivaji was exceptional with the ball, taking 2 for 18 in his 4 overs while also contributing 31 with the bat. Chani's 43 provided the batting backbone, and his opening spell of 4-0-19-1 kept the Blasters in check. B.Sasidhar's 2 wickets for 35 runs, including the decisive Mankad, nearly turned the game in their favor.
Turning Point – The Mankad Controversy

Without a doubt, the turning point of the match was B.Sasidhar's Mankad dismissal of Sagar Sharma in the final over. With just 2 runs needed off 3 balls, the non-striker's backing up too far proved costly. While such dismissals often spark debate about the spirit of cricket, within the laws of the game, it was a legitimate dismissal that turned the match briefly in Forever 11's favor. However, Sai Murali's cool-headed boundary on the very next ball ensured that Blasters of Bharat would not be denied their historic first chase.
A Historic First – The Maiden Successful Chase

This victory held special significance for Blasters of Bharat. It was their first successful run chase in the team's history. Previously, the team had relied on defending totals, but this match showed a new dimension to their game — the ability to handle pressure, chase down targets, and finish games under pressure.

For a team that had recently endured a humiliating 30-run collapse, this chase represents a remarkable turnaround. The belief that they can successfully chase targets will serve them well in future matches. The composure shown in the final over, the depth in batting, and the hunger to win were all on display.
What This Victory Means for Blasters of Bharat

This win goes beyond the scorecard. It's about proving to themselves that they can handle the pressure of a run chase. It's about the batsmen stepping up when the game was on the line. It's about staying alert for game-changing moments like the Mankad, but more importantly, it's about not letting such moments derail the chase. Sai Murali's boundary off the very next ball after the controversial dismissal showed the mental strength of this young side.

Most importantly, this victory is about building a winning culture after recent setbacks. From the lows of being bowled out for 30 to chasing down 141 with three wickets in hand, Blasters of Bharat have shown remarkable resilience. The team is finding its identity, and this historic chase could be the turning point of their season.

For Forever 11, this was a bitter pill to swallow — coming so close to victory only to fall short in the final over despite a controversial dismissal going their way. But such losses often become the foundation for future success. The experience of defending a total under pressure will be invaluable for their development.
Looking Ahead

With this historic first successful chase under their belt, Blasters of Bharat will look to build on this momentum. The team has shown they can compete, they can defend totals, and now they have proven they can chase as well. The future looks bright for this determined side as they continue to grow.
Full Match Scorecard

title: The Collapse of 30 Runs: Blazing Thunder Demolish Blasters of Bharat
author: BOB Admin Team
description: In a stunning display of bowling dominance, Blazing Thunder bowled out Blasters of Bharat for just 30 runs, securing a massive 153-run victory after posting 183/9.

content.value: A Collapse for the Record Books

Cricket is a game of glorious uncertainties, but what unfolded on the field was nothing short of a nightmare for Blasters of Bharat. Chasing a challenging target of 184, the batting lineup crumbled like a house of cards, collapsing to a humiliating 30 all out in just 8.1 overs. Blazing Thunder secured a monumental 153-run victory, delivering a performance that will be remembered for its sheer bowling dominance.
A Team in Transition

This match marked a significant moment for Blasters of Bharat — it was their second outing under new captain Sagar Sharma. The team is in a phase of transition, looking to rebuild and find its identity after a period of change. New leadership often brings fresh energy and new ideas, but it also comes with its own set of challenges. The players are still adjusting to the new dynamics, understanding the captain's vision, and finding their roles within the squad.

For any team undergoing transition, the first few matches are always the toughest. There is no substitute for time and experience together. While the result on the scoreboard was far from ideal, these early challenges are often the building blocks for something stronger in the future. Every great team has faced setbacks on their journey, and how they respond to adversity defines their character.
First Innings – Pannu's Explosive Knock Sets the Stage

Batting first, Blazing Thunder posted a commanding 183/9, a total built around a breathtaking innings from Ankush Pannu. Walking in after early setbacks, Pannu launched a relentless assault on the Blasters' bowlers, smashing 74 runs off just 39 balls. His knock was laced with 4 boundaries and a staggering 7 sixes, leaving the opposition searching for answers.

Galat launda provided the perfect finishing touch with a quickfire 30 off 15 balls, while a vital 30-run partnership for the final wicket ensured the Thunder crossed the 180-run mark — a total that would prove to be far beyond the reach of the chasing side.
The Chase – A Nightmare Begins

If the target seemed daunting, the start was catastrophic. Ankush Pannu, already the hero with the bat, turned destroyer with the ball. In the very first over, he removed both openers — Sachin Mishra and Gyan Kumar — for ducks. The Blasters of Bharat were reeling at 2/2, and the collapse had only just begun.

Wickets continued to tumble like dominoes. Vineet Saini joined the party, dismissing Pavan K Reddy, and then Manju and Mohinesh Sharma ripped through the middle and lower order. The scoreboard told a harrowing story: 2/2, 3/4, 3/5, 8/6, 9/7, 9/8. The Blasters' batters had no answer to the relentless pressure under their new captain's first real test.
A Fleeting Glimmer in the Darkness

At 9/8, the Blasters of Bharat were staring at the possibility of being bowled out for a single-digit total. Then came Vidhya Sagar, the number 10 batter, who decided to go down swinging. In a brief but entertaining cameo, Sagar smashed 12 runs off just 5 balls, including a boundary and a towering six. His defiance helped the team limp past the 30-run mark.

But it was a mere consolation. The final wicket fell on the first ball of the 9th over, with Partha taking the catch to seal the emphatic victory. The Blasters of Bharat were bowled out for 30 — a total that summed up a complete batting collapse on a day when nothing went according to plan.
The Numbers That Tell the Story

The bowling figures for Blazing Thunder read like a dream. Manju finished with 2 wickets for just 2 runs. Mohinesh Sharma claimed 2 wickets for 6 runs in his 2 overs, including a maiden. Ankush Pannu's opening burst yielded 2 wickets for 2 runs. Vineet Saini added 2 wickets to his name. It was a collective bowling masterclass that left the opposition with no breathing room.
Turning Point – The First Over

The match was effectively decided in the very first over. Ankush Pannu's twin strikes sent shockwaves through the Blasters' dressing room. The psychological blow of losing both openers for a duck set the stage for the complete batting collapse that followed. From that moment, the chase was never about the required run rate — it was about survival, and the Blasters simply couldn't survive.
A Setback on the Road to Something Good

For Blasters of Bharat, this 30-run collapse is undoubtedly a painful setback. But in the journey of any team, especially one in transition under new leadership, such defeats often become the foundation for future success. New captain Sagar Sharma and his young team will take valuable lessons from this experience — understanding the importance of partnerships, the need for composure under pressure, and the reality of how quickly a game can turn.

As the saying goes, sometimes you have to fall before you can rise. This result, as harsh as it is, could be the wake-up call that sparks a turnaround. The true measure of this team will not be defined by one bad day, but by how they respond, regroup, and rebuild from here. For a team finding its feet under new leadership, this setback could very well be the foundation for something good on the horizon.
A Victory to Remember for Blazing Thunder

For Blazing Thunder, this victory was a statement. It showcased their ability to post a commanding total and then back it up with a ruthless, disciplined bowling performance. Ankush Pannu emerged as the undisputed star, excelling with both bat and ball. The supporting cast — Galat launda, Manju, Mohinesh Sharma, and Vineet Saini — played their roles to perfection.
Full Match Scorecard

title: Blasters of Bharat First Match Victory
author: BOB Admin Team
description: A memorable first victory that marked the beginning of Blasters of Bharat’s journey.

content.value: The Beginning of Something Special

What started as just another weekend plan turned into a memorable moment for Blasters of Bharat. A group of engineers stepped onto the ground not just to play, but unknowingly to create their first big victory together.
First Innings – A Tough Start
The innings didn’t begin as expected. Early wickets put the team under pressure, and scoring runs wasn’t easy in the beginning. Sagar Sharma fell early, and the team had to rebuild carefully.
Despite the early setbacks, players like Gyan Kumar and Saurabh Mishra stepped up to stabilize the innings and keep the scoreboard moving.
Building Momentum
As the innings progressed, the team started gaining confidence. Partnerships were built, singles were rotated well, and occasional boundaries helped maintain momentum.
Kushagra Singh and others contributed crucial runs in the middle overs, ensuring the team reached a competitive total.
Captain’s Impact – Gyan Kumar

Gyan Kumar led the team with calmness and intent in this match. As captain, he not only contributed with the bat but also kept the team motivated during tough phases. His decisions on the field, especially while rotating bowlers and setting the field, played an important role in maintaining pressure on the opposition.
For a team that was still finding its rhythm, Gyan’s leadership brought stability and confidence. He ensured everyone stayed focused and played as a unit.
Final Overs – Strong Finish
The last few overs brought in valuable runs. Smart batting, quick running between wickets, and timely boundaries helped Blasters of Bharat finish strong and set a defendable target.
Second Innings – Bowling Under Pressure
Defending the total was the real challenge. The bowlers stepped up with discipline, maintaining tight lines and not allowing easy runs.
Harsha Vardhana P, Karthik XVII, and R Goutham played key roles by taking crucial wickets and controlling the flow of runs.
Turning Point of the Match
The match turned in Blasters’ favor when two quick wickets fell in the middle overs. This shifted the pressure onto the opposition, increasing the required run rate and breaking their momentum.
Closing the Game
In the final overs, the team showed great composure. Tight bowling, sharp fielding, and smart decisions ensured that the opposition couldn’t recover.
Blasters of Bharat secured their first victory — a moment that marked the real beginning of their journey.
More Than Just a Win
This wasn’t just about winning a match. It was about teamwork, learning, and building something together. From a group of friends to a proper team, this victory gave everyone confidence and belief.
Blasters of Bharat is not just about cricket — it’s about passion, friendship, and the memories we create every weekend.
Full Match Scorecard

title: The Journey of Blasters of Bharat
author: BOB Admin Team
description: How a group of friends turned weekend cricket into a proper 20-over journey.

content.value: Where It All Started

Blasters of Bharat started back in 14th January 2025, not as a big team but just a small group of 3-4 friends who simply loved playing cricket. Like most weekend cricketers, we used to play on local grounds where multiple teams shared the same space, making it chaotic and sometimes frustrating.
Getting proper time to bat or bowl was always a challenge. Matches were unstructured, and the experience was far from the kind of cricket we actually wanted to play.
The Big Shift to 20 Overs

That’s when we decided to do something different — play proper 20-over matches. Instead of adjusting on crowded local grounds, we started exploring grounds on the outskirts of Bangalore. We booked proper grounds, planned matches, and began playing like a real team.

The first match felt different. Then came the second, and slowly, we realized how much we were enjoying this structured format. It wasn’t just casual cricket anymore — it started feeling like a real journey.
Leadership and Learning

Sachin, our first captain, played a huge role in shaping the team during the early days. Moving from short 7-over matches to full 20-over games is not easy — it requires planning, patience, and better decision-making.

He learned and adapted quickly, managing the team, handling match situations, and building a proper structure around how we play. That transition phase was challenging, but it helped us grow as a team.
More Than Just Matches
What started as a simple plan to play better cricket slowly turned into something much bigger. It became about team bonding, weekend excitement, and creating memories on the field.
Blasters of Bharat is not just a cricket team — it’s a journey of friends, passion, and the love for the game that keeps growing with every match we play.
